hypothesis: If a generative materials model truly captures chemically valid crystal structures, then its samples and lightly perturbed references will relax under DFT to low-RMSD configurations with energy-above-hull ≤0.1 eV/atom, low EMD distances, and high latent precision/recall, whereas systematically increasing perturbations or using weaker models will monotonically worsen these metrics because physically stable structures relax to nearby minima on the potential-energy surface.
